generatedSentence: Dr. Aris Thorne, an exhausted orthopedic surgeon, spent his day in a rural clinic, where knobbly trees stood sentinel. The clinic's interior, filled with old medical books, held a certain funky charm. He wrestled with mounting personal debts through long winters, his professional objectives feeling like an overgrown path. A digital vignette of the cyber frontier sparked an awakening inside him, urging him to involve himself. He received a cryptic message via a secure gateway cable, demanding he flip specific switches to connect to the omega network. This was no ordinary day for the man who once admired the intricate folkway of corn cultivation, now contemplating an amberjack-colored future. Kael, the vain corporate master, exerted his crown-like control over the city's data. His company's firewall was notoriously tricky, designed to dominate all information flow. Kael's politic maneuvers often backfired, his PR team quick to vilify any troublemaker. Thorne found himself under intense scrutiny from different departments, his efforts feeling like an icky dribble downwards against a raging torrent. The tension was crackling, his anxiety jumpy, as Kael’s media presence would roar with warnings, dismissing Thorne's efforts as a mere token gesture. There were even rumors of a digital hickey on the system, officially denied, that hinted at Kael's use of unstable magnesium in their servers. Deep within a repurposed inhouse server farm, Thorne felt an ecstatic surge, a flame of determination. His cyber contraptions, resembling cartoon backpacks and a makeshift data parachute, were ready. This was no mere personal hiatus; he had to join the fight, to level up his game. The system's encryption was formidable, an invisible airlock, a complex apostrophe in the digital code. He prepared a custom patch, a digital buckshot aimed at the network’s core. He had to reuse some old techniques, even risk an outtake from his past failures. He connected a video feed, knowing his actions would adumbrate the next phase, resisting the urge to upsell the false promises of digital wyverns. The thought brought a sense of calmer candor. As the system accepted his patch, a final flicker of data, Thorne felt his knuckled elbows ache. He'd lived a life like a snail, but now he had faced his frontier. The old rickety apartment he called home, a simple rectangle of concrete, suddenly seemed like a castle of triumph. He felt a vibrating energy, a jolly lightness in his chest, as if his very ventricle had been cleared. The digital threat that had suckled on the system was now sucked into oblivion. He could finally admire the larch trees outside, the simple beauty of a day that felt like a nickel coin, hard-earned and real. He had been a digital knight, who had knocked on the gates of power and won. No more kvetching, no more fasting from a truly connected world. He even considered taking up sporting events again, finding a new joint to celebrate his victory, perhaps even a washcloth to wipe away the grime of battle.

rawPanelSentences: Dr. Aris Thorne, an **exhausted** **orthopedic** surgeon, spent his **day** in a **rural** clinic, where **knobbly** trees stood sentinel. The clinic's interior, filled with old medical **book**s, held a certain **funky** charm. He wrestled with mounting personal **debts** through long **winters**, his professional **objectives** feeling like an **overgrown** path. A digital **vignette** of the **cyber** **frontier** sparked an **awakening** **inside** him, urging him to **involve** himself. He received a cryptic **message** via a secure **gateway** **cable**, demanding he flip specific **switches** to connect to the **omega** network. This was no ordinary **day** for the man who once admired the intricate **folkway** of **corn** cultivation, now contemplating an **amberjack**-colored future.

Kael, the **vain** corporate **master**, exerted his **crown**-like control over the city's data. His company's **firewall** was notoriously **tricky**, designed to **dominate** all information flow. Kael's **politic** maneuvers often **backfire**d, his PR team quick to **vilify** any **troublemaker**. Thorne found himself under intense **scrutiny** from **different** **departments**, his efforts feeling like an **icky** **dribble** **downwards** against a raging torrent. The tension was **crackling**, his anxiety **jumpy**, as Kael’s media presence would **roar** with warnings, dismissing Thorne's efforts as a mere **token** gesture. There were even rumors of a digital **hickey** on the system, **officially** denied, that hinted at Kael's use of unstable **magnesium** in their servers.

Deep within a repurposed **inhouse** server farm, Thorne felt an **ecstatic** surge, a **flame** of determination. His **cyber** contraptions, resembling **cartoon** **backpacks** and a makeshift data **parachute**, were ready. This was no mere personal **hiatus**; he had to **join** the fight, to **level up** his game. The system's **encryption** was formidable, an invisible **airlock**, a complex **apostrophe** in the digital code. He prepared a custom **patch**, a digital **buckshot** aimed at the network’s **core**. He had to **reuse** some old techniques, even risk an **outtake** from his past failures. He connected a **video** feed, knowing his actions would **adumbrate** the next phase, resisting the urge to **upsell** the false promises of digital **wyverns**. The thought brought a sense of **calmer** **candor**.

As the system accepted his **patch**, a final **flicker** of data, Thorne felt his **knuckled** **elbows** ache. He'd lived a life like a **snail**, but now he had faced his **frontier**. The old **rickety** **apartment** he called home, a simple **rectangle** of concrete, suddenly seemed like a **castle** of triumph. He felt a **vibrating** energy, a **jolly** lightness in his chest, as if his very **ventricle** had been cleared. The digital threat that had **suckled** on the system was now **sucked** into oblivion. He could finally **admire** the **larch** trees outside, the simple beauty of a day that felt like a **nickel** coin, hard-earned and real. He had been a digital **knight**, who had **knocked** on the gates of power and won. No more **kvetch**ing, no more **fasting** from a truly connected world. He even considered taking up **sporting** events again, finding a new **joint** to celebrate his victory, perhaps even a **washcloth** to wipe away the grime of battle.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neo-noir comic style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. An aging man with a salt-and-pepper beard, wearing a rumpled lab coat over a worn turtleneck, deep lines around his eyes, and a determined expression. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Dr. Aris Thorne sits hunched over a cluttered desk in a dim, rural clinic, staring intently at a flickering monitor displaying complex code, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Kael, a sharp-suited, stern-faced CEO with slicked-back gray hair and a faint scar above his eyebrow, leans back in a luxurious office chair, a menacing smirk on his face as he overlooks a holographic city map, low angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Dr. Aris Thorne, sweating with concentration, frantically connecting glowing cables to a makeshift server rig, resembling advanced backpack technology, in a stark, neon-lit server room, close-up on his hands and intense facial expression. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Dr. Aris Thorne stands on a rickety apartment balcony, gazing calmly at a cityscape at dawn, a faint, contented smile on his face, hands resting on the railing, medium shot. All human characters are visibly adult with mature features. cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. NO text, NO speech bubbles, NO dialogue, NO children.

definitions.definition: The top part of something:

Splendor; culmination; acme.

Any currency (originally) issued by the crown (regal power) and often bearing a crown (headdress); (translation) various currencies known by similar names in their native languages, such as the koruna, kruna, krone, korona.
A former pre-decimalization British coin worth five shillings.
The part of a plant where the root and stem meet.

In England, a standard size of printing paper measuring 20 × 15 inches.
In American, a standard size of writing paper measuring 19 × 15 inches.

A rounding or smoothing of the barrel opening
The area enclosed between two concentric perimeters.
A round spot shaved clean on the top of the head, as a mark of the clerical state; the tonsure.
A whole bird with the legs and wings removed to produce a joint of white meat.
A formal hat worn by women to Sunday church services; a church crown.
The knurled knob or dial, on the outside of a watch case, used to wind it or adjust the hands.
To place a crown on the head of.
To formally declare (someone) a king, queen, emperor, etc.

definitions.definition: To divert; to amuse; to make merry.
To represent by any kind of play.
To practise the diversions of the field or the turf; to be given to betting, as upon races.
To assume suddenly a new and different character from the rest of the plant or from the type of the species; said of a bud, shoot, plant, or animal.
To close (a door).
The act of taking part in a sport.
Pertaining to sports
definitions.example: He got a job in a sporting goods store.

definitions.definition: Exhibiting sportsmanship.
definitions.example: Quite sporting of you to call that foul on yourself.

definitions.definition: Having a reasonable chance of success.
definitions.example: You think he has a sporting chance? I wouldn't call even him a long shot!

definitions.definition: Fair, generous; ‘game’.
definitions.example: It was very sporting of her to let us off like that.

definitions.definition: (obsolete) Of or relating to unseemly male excesses, especially gambling, prostitution, or similar recreational activities.
A bundle of thin sticks, typically made of wood, sometimes bond in such a way that binding can be moved so that it varies the tightness of the binding.
A device to turn electric current on and off or direct its flow.
A change or exchange.
A movable section of railroad track which allows the train to be directed down one of two destination tracks; (set of) points.
A slender woody plant stem used as a whip; a thin, flexible rod, associated with corporal punishment in the United States.
A command line notation allowing specification of optional behavior.
definitions.example: Use the /b switch to specify black-and-white printing.

definitions.definition: A programming construct that takes different actions depending on the value of an expression.
definitions.example: 2004, "Curt", Can I use IF statements, and still use switches? (on newsgroup microsoft.public.word.mailmerge.fields)

definitions.definition: A networking device connecting multiple wires, allowing them to communicate simultaneously, when possible. Compare to the less efficient hub device that solely duplicates network packets to each wire.
A system of specialized relays, computer hardware, or other equipment which allows the interconnection of a calling party's telephone line with any called party's line.
A mechanism within DNA that activates or deactivates a gene.
(BDSM) One who is willing to take either a submissive or a dominant role in a sexual relationship.
A separate mass or tress of hair, or of some substance (such as jute) made to resemble hair, formerly worn on the head by women.
To exchange.
definitions.example: I want to switch this red dress for a green one.

definitions.definition: To change (something) to the specified state using a switch.
definitions.example: Switch the light on.

definitions.definition: To whip or hit with a switch.
To change places, tasks, etc.
definitions.example: I want to switch to a different seat.

definitions.definition: To get angry suddenly; to quickly or unreasonably become enraged.
To swing or whisk.
definitions.example: to switch a cane

definitions.definition: To be swung or whisked.
definitions.example: The angry cat's tail switched back and forth.

definitions.definition: To trim.
definitions.example: to switch a hedge

definitions.definition: To turn from one railway track to another; to transfer by a switch; generally with off, from, etc.
definitions.example: to switch off a train; to switch a car from one track to another

definitions.definition: To shift to another circuit.
Any period of 24 hours.
definitions.example: I've been here for two days and a bit.

definitions.definition: A period from midnight to the following midnight.
definitions.example: The day begins at midnight.

definitions.definition: Rotational period of a planet (especially Earth).
definitions.example: A day on Mars is slightly over 24 hours.

definitions.definition: The part of a day period which one spends at one’s job, school, etc.
definitions.example: I worked two days last week.

definitions.definition: Part of a day period between sunrise and sunset where one enjoys daylight; daytime.
definitions.example: day and night;  I work at night and sleep during the day.

definitions.definition: A specified time or period; time, considered with reference to the existence or prominence of a person or thing; age; time.
definitions.example: Every dog has its day.

definitions.definition: A period of contention of a day or less.
definitions.example: The day belonged to the Allies.

definitions.definition: A 24-hour period beginning at 6am or sunrise.
definitions.example: Your 8am forecast: The high for the day will be 30 and the low, before dawn, will be 10.

definitions.definition: To spend a day (in a place).
An intersection of piping or wiring; an interconnect.
An intersection of data in two or more database tables.
The act of joining something, such as a network.
The lowest upper bound, an operation between pairs of elements in a lattice, denoted by the symbol ∨.
To connect or combine into one; to put together.
definitions.example: The plumber joined the two ends of the broken pipe.

definitions.definition: To come together; to meet.
definitions.example: Parallel lines never join.

definitions.definition: To come into the company of.
definitions.example: I will join you watching the football game as soon as I have finished my work.

definitions.definition: To become a member of.
definitions.example: Many children join a sports club.

definitions.definition: To produce an intersection of data in two or more database tables.
definitions.example: By joining the Customer table on the Product table, we can show each customer's name alongside the products they have ordered.

definitions.definition: To unite in marriage.
To enjoin upon; to command.
To accept, or engage in, as a contest.
definitions.example: to join encounter, battle, or issue

definitions.definition: One who causes trouble, especially one who does so deliberately.
A complainer.
A person from the countryside; a rustic.
Relating to the countryside or to agriculture.
A bruise-like mark made during petting by pressing the mouth to the skin on one’s partner’s body and sucking.
An object whose name is unknown or cannot be recalled.

A printing defect caused by foreign matter on the printing surface resulting in a ring where the ink is missing, appearing as a spot of ink surrounded by a halo, or as an unprinted spot within a solid printed area.
A serif or other ornamentation on type.
Local swelling in a tissue area, especially skin, often due to injury.
A tool for making smooth, semicircular bends in conduit and pipe.
An unintended negative outcome or loss in regards to a deal or action, often preceded by the words "taking a ..." or "took a ... ".
definitions.example: Farmer Jones took a hickey on his soybean crop when the flood washed the seeds away.

definitions.definition: To grow beyond one's boundaries or containment, or beyond the proper size.
To grow over; (of one thing) to cause (a second thing) to become overgrown (with or by the first thing).
Having large numbers of plants which have become too big, and are hence spoiling the picturesqueness of a garden.
Having grown bigger without developing maturity or changing character.
definitions.example: He spends so much money on new technology: he's like an overgrown schoolboy buying toys.

definitions.definition: A material object that physically exists.
A goal that is striven for.
(grammar) The objective case.
(grammar) a noun or pronoun in the objective case.
The lens or lenses of a camera, microscope, or other optical device closest to the object being examined.
In an official manner; according to official rules or regulations.
Thoroughly, completely.
definitions.example: That’s it! I’m officially outraged.

definitions.definition: Often plural: a belief or custom common to members of a culture or society.
The main cereal plant grown for its grain in a given region, such as oats in parts of Scotland and Ireland, and wheat or barley in England and Wales.
Maize, a grain crop of the species Zea mays.
A grain or seed, especially of a cereal crop.
definitions.example: He paid her the nominal fee of two corns of barley.

definitions.definition: A small, hard particle.
To granulate; to form a substance into grains
definitions.example: to corn gunpowder

definitions.definition: To preserve using coarse salt, e.g. corned beef
To provide with corn (typically maize; or, in Scotland, oats) for feed
definitions.example: Corn the horses.

definitions.definition: To render intoxicated
definitions.example: ale strong enough to corn one

definitions.definition: Any small cavity within a body; a hollow part or organ, especially:
A running ornament consisting of leaves and tendrils, used in Gothic architecture.
A decorative design, originally representing vine branches or tendrils, at the head of a chapter, of a manuscript or printed book, or in a similar position.
(by extension) Any small borderless picture in a book, especially an engraving, photograph, or the like, which vanishes gradually at the edge.
(by extension) A short story or anecdote that presents a scene or tableau, or paints a picture.
The central pictorial image on a postage stamp.

The characteristic of a camera lens, either by deficiency in design or by mismatch of the lens with the film format, to produce an image smaller than the film's frame with a crudely focused border. Photographers may deliberately choose this characteristic for a special effect.
A small sticker affixed to a vehicle windscreen to indicate that tolls have been paid.
To make, as an engraving or a photograph, with a border or edge gradually fading away.
To shake with small, rapid movements to and fro.
To resonate.
definitions.example: Her mind was vibrating with excitement.

definitions.definition: To brandish; to swing to and fro.
definitions.example: to vibrate a sword or a staff

definitions.definition: To mark or measure by moving to and fro.
definitions.example: a pendulum vibrating seconds

definitions.definition: To affect with vibratory motion; to set in vibration.
To please or impress someone.
To use vibrato.
That vibrates
Any of several large food and game yellowtail fishes of the genus Seriola, found in warm waters of all oceans.
The text character ’, which serves as a punctuation mark in various languages and as a diacritical mark in certain rare contexts.
To cause to become awake.
definitions.example: She awakened him by ringing the bell.

definitions.definition: To stop sleeping; awake.
definitions.example: Each morning he awakens with a smile on his face.

definitions.definition: To bring into action (something previously dormant); to stimulate.
definitions.example: Awaken your entrepreneurial spirit!

definitions.definition: To call to a sense of sin.
The act of awaking, or ceasing to sleep.
A revival of religion, or more general attention to religious matters than usual.
Being roused into action or activity.
Rousing from sleep, in a natural or a figurative sense; rousing into activity; exciting
definitions.example:
the awakening city
 an awakening discourse
the awakening dawn.

definitions.definition: The act of forming, causing, or constituting; workmanship; construction.
Process of growth or development.
definitions.example: As a child, he didn’t seem like a genius in the making.

definitions.definition: A communication, or what is communicated; any concept or information conveyed.
definitions.example: We've just received an urgent message from the President.

definitions.definition: An underlying theme or conclusion to be drawn from something.
definitions.example: The main message of the novel is that time heals all wounds.

definitions.definition: (chiefly in the plural) An errand.
See messages.
To send a message to; to transmit a message to, e.g. as text via a cell phone.
definitions.example: I messaged her about the concert.

definitions.definition: To send (something) as a message; usually refers to electronic messaging.
definitions.example: Please message the final report by fax.

definitions.definition: To send a message or messages; to be capable of sending messages.
definitions.example: The runaway computer program was messaging non-stop.

definitions.definition: To bear as a message.
The interior or inner part.
definitions.example: The inside of the building has been extensively restored.

definitions.definition: The left-hand side of a road if one drives on the left, or right-hand side if one drives on the right.
definitions.example: On a motorway, you should never pass another vehicle on the inside.

definitions.definition: The side of a curved road, racetrack etc. that has the shorter arc length; the side of a racetrack nearer the interior of the course or some other point of reference.
definitions.example: The car in front drifted wide on the bend, so I darted up the inside to take the lead.

definitions.definition: (in the plural) The interior organs of the body, especially the guts.
definitions.example: Eating that stuff will damage your insides.

definitions.definition: A passenger within a coach or carriage, as distinguished from one upon the outside.
Of or pertaining to the inner surface, limit or boundary.
definitions.example: The inside surface of the cup is unpainted.

definitions.definition: Nearer to the interior or centre of something.
definitions.example: All the window seats were occupied, so she took an inside seat.

definitions.definition: Originating from, arranged by, or being someone inside an organisation.
definitions.example: The reporter had received inside information about the forthcoming takeover.

definitions.definition: (of a person) Legally married to or related to (e.g. born in wedlock to), and/or residing with, a specified other person (parent, child, or partner); (of a marriage, relationship, etc) existing between two such people.
(of a pitch) Toward the batter as it crosses home plate.
definitions.example: The first pitch is ... just a bit inside.

definitions.definition: At or towards or the left-hand side of the road if one drives on the left, or right-hand side if one drives on the right.
definitions.example: the inside lane of the motorway

definitions.definition: Within or towards the interior of something; within the scope or limits of something (a place), especially a building.
definitions.example: It started raining, so I went inside.

definitions.definition: Indoors.
definitions.example: It was snowing, so the children stayed inside.

definitions.definition: Intimately, secretly; without expressing what one is feeling or thinking.
definitions.example: Are you laughing at us inside?

definitions.definition: Within the interior of something, closest to the center or to a specific point of reference.
definitions.example: He placed the letter inside the envelope.

definitions.definition: Within a period of time.
definitions.example: The job was finished inside two weeks.

definitions.definition: To roll or fold up; to wind round; to entwine.
To envelop completely; to surround; to cover; to hide.
definitions.example: to involve in darkness or obscurity

definitions.definition: To complicate or make intricate, as in grammatical structure.
To connect with something as a natural or logical consequence or effect; to include necessarily; to imply.
To take in; to gather in; to mingle confusedly; to blend or merge.
To envelop, enfold, entangle.
definitions.example: He's involved in the crime.

definitions.definition: To engage (someone) to participate in a task.
definitions.example: By getting involved in her local community, Mary met lots of people and also helped make it a nicer place to live.

definitions.definition: To raise to any assigned power; to multiply, as a quantity, into itself a given number of times.
definitions.example: a quantity involved to the third or fourth power

definitions.definition: An action, state of mind, or object one has an obligation to perform for another, adopt toward another, or give to another.
The state or condition of owing something to another.
definitions.example: I am in your debt.

definitions.definition: Money that one person or entity owes or is required to pay to another, generally as a result of a loan or other financial transaction.
An action at law to recover a certain specified sum of money alleged to be due.

Traditionally the fourth of the four seasons, typically regarded as being from December 23 to March 20 in continental regions of the Northern Hemisphere or the months of June, July and August in the Southern Hemisphere. It is the time when the sun is lowest in the sky, resulting in short days, and the time of year with the lowest atmospheric temperatures for the region.
The period of decay, old age, death, or the like.
Someone with dark skin, eyes and hair, seen as best suited to certain colors of clothing.
An appliance to be fixed on the front of a grate, to keep a kettle warm, etc.
To spend the winter (in a particular place).
definitions.example: When they retired, they hoped to winter in Florida.

definitions.definition: To store something (for instance animals) somewhere over winter to protect it from cold.
In the winter.
definitions.example: They ski winters in the Laurentians.

definitions.definition: A small cloth used to wash the face and body.
A pattern of concentric circles.
A circle of three or more leaves, flowers, or other organs, about the same part or joint of a stem.
A volution, or turn, of the spire of a univalve shell.
Any volution, as for example in the human ear or fingerprint.
A flywheel, a weight attached to a spindle.
To form a pattern of concentric circles.
The joint between the upper arm and the forearm.

(by extension) Any turn or bend like that of the elbow, in a wall, building, coastline, etc.; an angular or jointed part of any structure, such as the raised arm of a chair or sofa, or a short pipe fitting, turning at an angle or bent.
definitions.example: the sides of windows, where the jamb makes an elbow with the window back

definitions.definition: A detective.
Part of a basketball court located at the intersection of the free-throw line and the free-throw lane.
A hit with the elbow.
To push with the elbow.
definitions.example: He elbowed his way through the crowd.

definitions.definition: (by extension) To nudge, jostle or push.
To draw or let out wholly; to drain off completely
definitions.example: Moisture of the earth is exhausted by evaporation.

definitions.definition: To empty by drawing or letting out the contents
definitions.example: to exhaust a treasury

definitions.definition: To drain; to use up or expend wholly, or until the supply comes to an end
definitions.example: I exhausted my strength walking up the hill.

definitions.definition: To tire out; to wear out; to cause to be without any energy
definitions.example: The marathon exhausted me.

definitions.definition: To bring out or develop completely
To discuss thoroughly or completely
definitions.example: That subject has already been fully exhausted.

definitions.definition: To subject to the action of various solvents in order to remove all soluble substances or extractives
definitions.example: to exhaust a drug successively with water, alcohol, and ether

definitions.definition: Very tired; in a state of exhaustion.
definitions.example: The exhausted man fell asleep immediately.

definitions.definition: Depleted of resources.
definitions.example: The exhausted mine was worthless once all the ore had been extracted.

definitions.definition: The process of obscuring information to make it unreadable without special knowledge, key files, or passwords.
A ciphertext, a cryptogram, an encrypted value. Usually used with the preposition "of" followed by the value that is hidden in it.
(in the plural) Transports of delight; words or actions performed in a state of ecstasy.
A person in a state of ecstasy.
Feeling or characterized by ecstasy.
Extremely happy.
Relating to, or caused by, ecstasy or excessive emotion.
definitions.example: ecstatic gaze; ecstatic trance

definitions.definition: To raise to the same level or status; to improve so as to become as good as others.
definitions.example: The government promises to level up the department.

definitions.definition: To progress to the next level of player character stats and abilities, often by acquiring experience points in role-playing games.
definitions.example: I leveled up after defeating the dragon.

definitions.definition: Of an object: not strong or sturdy, as because of poor construction or upkeep; not safe or secure.
definitions.example: He hesitated about climbing such a small, rickety ladder.

definitions.definition: Of a person: feeble in the joints; tottering.
definitions.example: The rickety old man hardly managed to climb the stairs.

definitions.definition: Affected with or suffering from rickets.
A gap in a series, making it incomplete.
An interruption, break or pause.
An unexpected break from work.
A gap in geological strata.
An opening in an organ.
definitions.example: Hiatus aorticus is an opening in the diaphragm through which aorta and thoracic duct pass.

definitions.definition: A syllable break between two vowels, without an intervening consonant. (Compare diphthong.)
definitions.example: Words like reality and naïve contain vowels in hiatus.

definitions.definition: The visible part of fire; a stream of burning vapour or gas, emitting light and heat.
A romantic partner or lover in a usually short-lived but passionate affair.
Intentionally insulting criticism or remark meant to incite anger.
A brilliant reddish orange-gold fiery colour.
The contrasting light and dark figure seen in wood used for stringed instrument making; the curl.
definitions.example: The cello has a two-piece back with a beautiful narrow flame.

definitions.definition: Burning zeal, passion, imagination, excitement, or anger.
To produce flames; to burn with a flame or blaze.
To burst forth like flame; to break out in violence of passion; to be kindled with zeal or ardour.
To post a destructively critical or abusive message (to somebody).
definitions.example: I flamed him for spamming in my favourite newsgroup.

definitions.definition: Of a brilliant reddish orange-gold colour, like that of a flame.
(of a person) Peaceful, quiet, especially free from anger and anxiety.
(of a place or situation) Free of noise and disturbance.
(of water) with few or no waves on the surface; not rippled.
Without wind or storm.
A person or thing that calms.
definitions.example: Christ was the calmer of a storm on the Sea of Galilee.

definitions.definition: Whiteness; brilliance; purity.
The state of being sincere and open in speech; honesty in expression.
Impartiality.
A small, controlled fire set in the path of a larger uncontrolled fire, in order to limit the spread of the large fire by removing its fuel.

An explosion produced either by a running internal combustion engine that occurs in the air intake or exhaust system rather than inside the combustion chamber or unburned fuel or hydrocarbons ignited somewhere in the exhaust system.
A premature explosion in the cylinder of a gas or oil engine during the exhaust or the compression stroke, tending to drive the piston in the wrong direction.
An explosion in the exhaust passages of an internal combustion engine.
(of a gun, cannon, Bunsen burner, etc.) To fire in the opposite direction, for example due to an obstruction in the barrel.
(of an engine) To experience a premature ignition of fuel or an ignition of exhaust gases, making a popping sound.
To fail in a manner that brings down further misfortune.
definitions.example: His attempt to make money by importing luxury cars backfired horribly when fuel prices tripled.

definitions.definition: A piece of cloth, or other suitable material, sewed or otherwise fixed upon a garment to repair or strengthen it, especially upon an old garment to cover a hole.
definitions.example: His sleeves had patches on the elbows where different fabric had been sewn on to replace material that had worn away.

definitions.definition: A small piece of anything used to repair damage or a breach; as, a patch on a kettle, a roof, etc.
definitions.example: I can't afford to replace the roof, which is what it really needs. I'll have the roofer apply a patch.

definitions.definition: A piece of any size, used to repair something for a temporary period only, or that it is temporary because it is not meant to last long or will be removed as soon as a proper repair can be made, which will happen in the near future.
definitions.example: "This patch should hold until you reach the city," the mechanic said as he patted the car's hood.

definitions.definition: A small, usually contrasting but always somehow different or distinct, part of something else (location, time, size)
definitions.example: Doesn't that patch of clouds looks like a bunny?

definitions.definition: (specifically) A small area, a small plot of land or piece of ground.
definitions.example: Scattered patches of trees or growing corn.

definitions.definition: A local region of professional responsibility.
A small piece of black silk stuck on the face or neck to heighten beauty by contrast, worn by ladies in the 17th and 18th centuries; an imitation beauty mark.
A piece of material used to cover a wound.
An adhesive piece of material, impregnated with a drug, which is worn on the skin, the drug being slowly absorbed over a period of time.
definitions.example: Many people use a nicotine patch to wean themselves off of nicotine.

definitions.definition: A cover worn over a damaged eye, an eyepatch.
definitions.example: He had scratched his cornea so badly that his doctor told him to wear a patch.

definitions.definition: A block on the muzzle of a gun, to do away with the effect of dispart, in sighting.
A patch file, a file that describes changes to be made to a computer file or files, usually changes made to a computer program that fix a programming bug.
A small piece of material that is manually passed through a gun barrel to clean it.
A piece of greased cloth or leather used as wrapping for a rifle ball, to make it fit the bore.
(often patch cable, patch cord etc.; see also patch panel) A cable connecting two pieces of electrical equipment.
A sound setting for a musical synthesizer (originally selected by means of a patch cable).
An overlay used to obtain a stronger impression.
To mend by sewing on a piece or pieces of cloth, leather, or the like
definitions.example: My coat needs patching.

definitions.definition: To mend with pieces; to repair by fastening pieces on.
To make out of pieces or patches, like a quilt.
To join or unite the pieces of; to patch the skirt.
To employ a temporary, removable electronic connection, as one between two components in a communications system.
(generally with the particle "up") To repair or arrange in a hasty or clumsy manner
definitions.example: The truce between the two countries has been patched up.

definitions.definition: To make the changes a patch describes; to apply a patch to the files in question. Hence:
To connect two pieces of electrical equipment using a cable.
definitions.example: I'll need to patch the preamp output to the mixer.

definitions.definition: Television, television show, movie.
A short film clip, with or without audio (as in a music video, or one of the plethora of user-generated short movies on sites such as YouTube).
Motion picture stored on VHS or some other format.
VHS.
To record using a video camera, to videotape
To record a television program
To say defamatory things about someone or something; to speak ill of.
To belittle through speech; to put down.
The act of storing goods; the state of being stored.
definitions.example: There's a lot of storage space in the loft.

definitions.definition: (usually countable) An object or place in which something is stored.
(usually uncountable) Any computer device, including such as a disk, on which data is stored for a longer term than memory.
definitions.example: I′d recommend backing up these files to storage before reinstalling the operating system.

definitions.definition: The price charged for storing goods.
To put into storage; to store.
Any of very many animals (either hermaphroditic or nonhermaphroditic), of the class Gastropoda, having a coiled shell.
(by extension) A slow person; a sluggard.
A spiral cam, or a flat piece of metal of spirally curved outline, used for giving motion to, or changing the position of, another part, as the hammer tail of a striking clock.
A tortoise or testudo; a movable roof or shed to protect besiegers.
The pod of the snail clover.
To move or travel very slowly.
definitions.example: The cars were snailing along the motorway during the rush hour.

definitions.definition: To give suck to; to nurse at the breast, udder, or dugs.
To nurse; to suck milk from a nursing mother.
To nurse from (a breast, nursing mother, etc.).
Intense study of someone or something.
Thorough inspection of a situation or a case.
An examination of catechumens, in the last week of Lent, who were to receive baptism on Easter Day.
A ticket, or little paper billet, on which a vote is written.
An examination by a committee of the votes given at an election, for the purpose of correcting the poll.
To scrutinize.
The different ideal.
Not the same; exhibiting a difference.
Various, assorted, diverse.
Distinct, separate; used for emphasis after numbers and other determiners of quantity.
definitions.example: Several different scientists all reached this conclusion at about the same time.

definitions.definition: Unlike most others; unusual.
Differently.
A part, portion, or subdivision.
A distinct course of life, action, study, or the like.
definitions.example: Technical things are not his department; he's a people person.

definitions.definition: A subdivision of an organization.

A territorial division; a district; especially, in France, one of the districts into which the country is divided for governmental purposes, similar to a county in the UK and in the USA. France is composed of 101 départements organized in 18 régions, each department is divided into arrondissements, in turn divided into cantons.
A military subdivision of a country
definitions.example: the Department of the Potomac

definitions.definition: Act of departing; departure.
The act of making such a sale.
To persuade a customer to buy more than he or she had intended.
A draconian creature possessing wings, only two legs and usually a barbed tail.
The act of salvaging or in some manner restoring a discarded item to yield something usable.
The act of using again, or in another place.
definitions.example: Code reuse can save programmers a lot of typing.

definitions.definition: To use again something that is considered past its usefulness (usually for something else).
definitions.example: The students reused empty plastic bottles in their science experiment.

definitions.definition: To use again, or in another place.
A portion of a recording (a take) that is not included in the final version of a film or a musical album, often because it contains a mistake.
definitions.example: The DVD for that movie has ten minutes worth of outtakes.

definitions.definition: A complete version of a recording or film that is dropped in favour of another version, reject.
An opening for outward discharge; a vent.
To take out, remove.
To except.
The part of a country which borders or faces another country or unsettled region
The most advanced or recent version of something; leading edge.
definitions.example: the frontier of civilization

definitions.definition: An outwork of a fortification.
To live as pioneers on frontier territory.
To place on the frontier.
An unsteady flash of light.
definitions.example: the flicker of a poorly tuned television set

definitions.definition: A short moment.
To burn or shine unsteadily, or with a wavering light.
To keep going on and off; to appear and disappear for short moments; to flutter.
To flutter; to flap the wings without flying.
A large building that is fortified and contains many defences; in previous ages often inhabited by a nobleman or king.
An instance of castling.
A rook; a chess piece shaped like a castle tower.
A defense structure in shogi formed by defensive pieces surrounding the king.
A close helmet.
Any strong, imposing, and stately mansion.
A small tower, as on a ship, or an elephant's back.
The wicket.
To house or keep in a castle.
To protect or separate in a similar way.
To make into a castle: to build in the form of a castle or add (real or imitation) battlements to an existing building.

(usually intransitive) To move the king 2 squares right or left and, in the same turn, the nearest rook to the far side of the king. The move now has special rules: the king cannot be in, go through, or end in check; the squares between the king and rook must be vacant; and neither piece may have been moved before castling.
(usually intransitive) To create a similar defensive position in Japanese chess through several moves.
To bowl a batsman with a full-length ball or yorker such that the stumps are knocked over.
The central part of fruit, containing the kernels or seeds.
definitions.example: the core of an apple or quince

definitions.definition: The heart or inner part of a physical thing
The center or inner part of a space or area
definitions.example: the core of a square

definitions.definition: The most important part of a thing; the essence.
definitions.example: the core of a subject

definitions.definition: The portion of a mold that creates an internal cavity within a casting or that makes a hole in or through a casting.
The bony process which forms the central axis of the horns in many animals.
Magnetic data storage.
An individual computer processor, in the sense when several processors (called cores or CPU cores) are plugged together in one single integrated circuit to work as one (called multi-core processor).
definitions.example: I wanted to play a particular computer game, which required I buy a new computer, so while the game said it needed at least a dual-core processor, I wanted my computer to be a bit ahead of the curve, so I bought a quad-core.

definitions.definition: The material between surface materials in a structured composite sandwich material.
definitions.example: a floor panel with a Nomex honeycomb core

definitions.definition: The inner part of a nuclear reactor in which the nuclear reaction takes place.
A piece of ferromagnetic material (i.e. soft iron), inside the windings of an electromagnet, that channels the magnetic field.
A disorder of sheep caused by worms in the liver.
A cylindrical sample of rock or other materials obtained by core drilling.
A tiny sample of organic material obtained by means of a fine-needle biopsy.
The central part of a protein structure consisting in mostly hydrophobic aminoacids.
The set of feasible allocations that cannot be improved upon by a subset (a coalition) of the economy's agents.
A hollow cylindrical piece of cardboard around which a web of paper or plastic is winded.
An atomic nucleus plus inner electrons (i.e. except valence electrons).
To remove the core of an apple or other fruit.
To extract a sample with a drill.
Lead shot used in shotgun cartridges
To foreshadow vaguely.
To give a vague outline.
To obscure or overshadow.
A complete domicile occupying only part of a building, especially one for rent; a flat.
A suite of rooms within a domicile, designated for a specific person or persons and including a bedroom.
A division of an enclosure that is separate from others; a compartment
(COM) A conceptual space used for separation in the threading architecture. Objects in one apartment cannot directly access those in another, but must use a proxy.
To be amazed at; to view with surprise; to marvel at.
To regard with wonder and delight.
To look upon with an elevated feeling of pleasure, as something which calls out approbation, esteem, love or reverence.
To estimate or value highly; to hold in high esteem.
definitions.example: He had always admired the work ethos and family values of his friend.

definitions.definition: To be enthusiastic about (doing something); to want or like (to do something). (Sometimes followed by to.)
Belonging to or part of an organization; internal.
To use the mouth and lips to pull in (a liquid, especially milk from the breast).
To perform such an action; to feed from a breast or teat.
To put the mouth or lips to (a breast, a mother etc.) to draw in milk.
To extract, draw in (a substance) from or out of something.
To work the lips and tongue on (an object) to extract moisture or nourishment; to absorb (something) in the mouth.
To pull (something) in a given direction, especially without direct contact.
To perform fellatio.
To be inferior or objectionable: a general term of disparagement, sometimes used with at to indicate a particular area of deficiency.
The point where two components of a structure join, but are still able to rotate.
definitions.example: This rod is free to swing at the joint with the platform.

definitions.definition: The point where two components of a structure join rigidly.
definitions.example: The water is leaking out of the joint between the two pipes.

definitions.definition: Any part of the body where two bones join, in most cases allowing that part of the body to be bent or straightened.
The means of securing together the meeting surfaces of components of a structure.
definitions.example: The dovetail joint, while more difficult to make, is also quite strong.

definitions.definition: A cut of meat.
definitions.example: Set the joint in a roasting tin and roast for the calculated cooking time.

definitions.definition: The part or space included between two joints, knots, nodes, or articulations.
definitions.example: a joint of cane or of a grass stem; a joint of the leg

definitions.definition: A fracture in which the strata are not offset; a geologic joint.
(somewhat derogatory) A place of business, particularly in the food service or hospitality industries.
definitions.example: It was the kind of joint you wouldn't want your boss to see you in.

definitions.definition: (with the definite article) Prison.
definitions.example: I'm just trying to stay out of the joint.

definitions.definition: A marijuana cigarette.
definitions.example: After locking the door and closing the shades, they lit the joint.

definitions.definition: A syringe used to inject an illicit drug.
The penis.
To unite by a joint or joints; to fit together; to prepare so as to fit together
definitions.example: a jointing plane

definitions.definition: To join; to connect; to unite; to combine.
To provide with a joint or joints; to articulate.
To separate the joints; of; to divide at the joint or joints; to disjoint; to cut up into joints, as meat.
To fit as if by joints; to coalesce as joints do.
definitions.example: the stones joint, neatly.

definitions.definition: Done by two or more people or organisations working together.
definitions.example: The play was a joint production between the two companies.

definitions.definition: A pleasure trip or excursion.
A marine in the English navy.
To amuse or divert.
Full of merriment and high spirits; jovial.
Full of life and mirth; jouous; merry.
Splendid, excellent, pleasant
Large
Very, extremely
Hard to deal with, complicated
definitions.example: They were in a tricky situation.

definitions.definition: Adept at using deception
definitions.example: A tricky salesman can sell anything.

definitions.definition: Relating to or associated with a prostitution trick
definitions.example: I don't want any of your tricky money, thank you!

definitions.definition: A coniferous tree, of genus Larix, having deciduous leaves, in fascicles.
The wood of the larch.
A quadrilateral having opposing sides parallel and four right angles.
To restrict one’s personal consumption, generally of food, but sometimes other things, in various manners (totally, temporally, by avoiding particular items), often for religious or medical reasons.
definitions.example: Muslims fast during Ramadan and Catholics during Lent.

definitions.definition: Abstinence from food
A young servant or follower; a trained military attendant in service of a lord.
A minor nobleman with an honourable military rank who had served as a page and squire.
(by extension) An armored and mounted warrior of the Middle Ages.
definitions.example: King Arthur and the Knights of the Round Table

definitions.definition: (modern) A person on whom a knighthood has been conferred by a monarch.
A brave, chivalrous and honorable man devoted to a noble cause or love interest.
A chess piece, often in the shape of a horse's head, that is moved two squares in one direction and one at right angles to that direction in a single move, leaping over any intervening pieces.
A playing card bearing the figure of a knight; the knave or jack.
Any of various nymphalid butterflies of the genus Ypthima.
(modern) A generic name for various mushrooms belonging to the fungal order gilled mushrooms; scientific name Tricholoma.
To strike for admittance; to rap upon, as a door.
To criticize verbally; to denigrate; to undervalue.
definitions.example: Don’t knock it until you’ve tried it.

definitions.definition: To kick a ball towards another player; to pass.
To impress forcibly or strongly; to astonish; to move to admiration or applause.
To bump or impact.
definitions.example: I accidentally knocked my drink off the bar.

definitions.definition: To rap one's knuckles against something, especially wood.
definitions.example: Knock on the door and find out if they’re home.

definitions.definition: A person who endlessly whines or complains; a person who finds fault with anything.
An instance of kvetching; a complaint or whine.
To whine or complain, often needlessly and incessantly.
To apply pressure, or rub or massage with one's knuckles.
definitions.example: He knuckled the sleep from his eyes.

definitions.definition: To bend the fingers.
To touch one's forehead as a mark of respect.
To yield.
(chiefly in combination) Having a certain type of knuckles.
